tool.name: union
tool.summary: Combine two or more sequence inputs into one deterministic output stream

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: `union` combines two or more local sequence inputs into one output stream by stable concatenation. The first Epithema release keeps the semantics intentionally simple: preserve input order, preserve per-input record order, and preserve duplicates exactly as they were read.

sections.title: Inputs
sections.content: The current v1 interface requires at least two local sequence input paths. Each input is loaded through the shared Epithema sequence readers for FASTA, FASTQ, EMBL, and GenBank.

sections.title: Outputs
sections.content: The tool emits one combined FASTA sequence collection through the shared output path. CLI output also includes the standard Epithema method summary lines reporting input count, output record count, ordering policy, duplicate policy, and FASTA output format.

sections.title: Current Status
sections.content: This method is implemented and exposed through `epithema union`. Validation currently covers two-input concatenation, duplicate-identifier preservation, too-few-input failure, and malformed or empty-input failure behavior.

sections.title: Caveats
sections.content: The first release does not perform identifier-based or content-based deduplication. Records that share identifiers, descriptions, or sequence content are preserved as independent output records in first-seen order.

artifacts.label: Three-record FASTA fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed FASTA fixture used as the leading input for deterministic union validation.

artifacts.label: Two-record FASTA fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed FASTA fixture appended after the three-record input during union validation.

examples.title: Concatenate two FASTA inputs in stable order
examples.description: Appends a two-record FASTA fixture after a three-record FASTA fixture and returns the five-record combined stream in deterministic order.

expected_outputs.label: Combined sequence collection
expected_outputs.description: A FASTA sequence collection containing the first input records followed by the second input records without deduplication.
